tossups.question: This author opened a poem with the line "Here's what that bastard calls the emptiness" in response to a criticism of his poetry as "unpeopled". This author describes "my pen's beak, plucking up wriggling insects / like nouns" in another poem, which likens the title birds to "the bleached regrets of an old man's memoirs". This poet compares an adulterer who hear's Nausicaa's name in "every gull's outcry" to a "sail which leans on light / tired of (*) islands" in a poem which concludes "The classics can console. But not enough". This author of White Egrets writes of "Kikuyu, quick as flies" in a poem which asks how he can choose "between this Africa and the English tongue I love?" The poems "Sea Grapes" and "A Far Cry from Africa" are by, for 10 points, what Nobel Laureate from St. Lucia?
tossups.answer: Derek Walcott (The first clue refers to a poem from White Egrets rebutting V.S. Naipaul's criticism.)
tossups.answer_sanitized: Derek Walcott (The first clue refers to a poem from White Egrets rebutting V.S. Naipaul's criticism.)

The poems "Sea Grapes" and "A Far Cry from Africa" are by, for 10 points, what Nobel Laureate from St. Lucia?

tossups.question: In a novel by this author, the protagonist plays squash at the Hellenic Club, where he often goes with a lover whom he beats and spits on before leaving for London. In that novel of his, a man sends the Youth Guard to do forced labor after they fail to promote his book of maxims. A character created by this author writes about “Deserving Destitutes” for The Sentinel and later gets a car while working as a Community Welfare officer. A protagonist of this author helps (*) Ferdinand, the son of the magician Zabeth, attend school and runs a shop with his assistant Metty in the Domain. This author of a novel set in an African country ruled by the “Big Man” created a sign painter who tries to escape the Tulsis by getting his own home. For 10 points, name this author of A Bend in the River and A House for Mr. Biswas.
tossups.question_sanitized: In a novel by this author, the protagonist plays squash at the Hellenic Club, where he often goes with a lover whom he beats and spits on before leaving for London. In that novel of his, a man sends the Youth Guard to do forced labor after they fail to promote his book of maxims. A character created by this author writes about "Deserving Destitutes" for The Sentinel and later gets a car while working as a Community Welfare officer. A protagonist of this author helps (*) Ferdinand, the son of the magician Zabeth, attend school and runs a shop with his assistant Metty in the Domain. This author of a novel set in an African country ruled by the "Big Man" created a sign painter who tries to escape the Tulsis by getting his own home. For 10 points, name this author of A Bend in the River and A House for Mr. Biswas.

tossups.question: In one of this author’s novels, Ganesh Ramsumair becomes a successful politician thanks to his ability to cure illness with his hands. In an interview, this author claimed he couldn’t possibly “share [Jane Austen’s] ($) sentimental ambitions, her sentimental sense of the world” before getting to the point that no female writer was his equal. The father of one of this author’s protagonists drowns after his son (*) hides following a cow’s death. The “State Domain” is built in the title location under the command of the Big Man, in another one of this author’s novels that is narrated by Salim. For ten points, identify this author of The Mystic Masseur, A House for Mr. Biswas, and A Bend in the River.
tossups.question_sanitized: In one of this author's novels, Ganesh Ramsumair becomes a successful politician thanks to his ability to cure illness with his hands. In an interview, this author claimed he couldn't possibly "share [Jane Austen's] ($) sentimental ambitions, her sentimental sense of the world" before getting to the point that no female writer was his equal. The father of one of this author's protagonists drowns after his son (*) hides following a cow's death. The "State Domain" is built in the title location under the command of the Big Man, in another one of this author's novels that is narrated by Salim. For ten points, identify this author of The Mystic Masseur, A House for Mr. Biswas, and A Bend in the River.

bonuses.leadin: In response to the desertion of his wife Emelda, this character becomes a belligerent drunk, which ironically earns him the respect of his peers. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this carpenter who gives up on making the “thing without a name” after he is imprisoned for theft. This carpenter appears in a collection that also features the prophet Man-Man and the poet B. Wordsworth.
Although there is no Mr. Popo, this classic novel by Wu Cheng’en about Xuanzang’s pilgrimage to India does have characters named the Ox King and Son Goku.

The protagonist of this Natsume Soseki novel takes a job as a math teacher on Shikoku, where he works alongside “Radish” and “Squash.” This novel depicts the title character’s struggle with the teacher “Red Shirt.”
answers: Journey to the West [or Xi You Ji; accept Monkey]
Botchan (The theme of this bonus is “literary characters who share their name with DBZ characters.” Grumble if you will—Hippocleides doesn’t care.)
answers_sanitized: Mr. Popo (from Miguel Street by V.S. Naipaul)
Journey to the West [or Xi You Ji; accept Monkey]
Botchan (The theme of this bonus is "literary characters who share their name with DBZ characters." Grumble if you will-Hippocleides doesn't care.)

parts_sanitized: Name this carpenter who gives up on making the "thing without a name" after he is imprisoned for theft. This carpenter appears in a collection that also features the prophet Man-Man and the poet B. Wordsworth.
Although there is no Mr. Popo, this classic novel by Wu Cheng'en about Xuanzang's pilgrimage to India does have characters named the Ox King and Son Goku.

The protagonist of this Natsume Soseki novel takes a job as a math teacher on Shikoku, where he works alongside "Radish" and "Squash." This novel depicts the title character's struggle with the teacher "Red Shirt."

bonuses.leadin: This poet’s “Christmas in India” is used to exemplify the Eurocentric “representative” stage of writing native literature for an English audience in the post-colonial study “The Empire Writes Back.” For 10 points each:
parts: Name this British poet whose alignment with colonizers is reflected in the poem “The White Man’s Burden.”

This author cited the idea of post-colonial authors “writing back” against British colonialism in “The Empire Writes Back with a Vengeance.” This author created the fictional Pakistani town of Q in the novel Shame.

As an example of using untranslated words in English literature to “write back,” the study examines this short story collection’s use of the word “hubshi” to describe a Black maid whom Santosh marries to gain citizenship after he immigrates to Washington, D.C. in the story “One Out of Many.”
answers_sanitized: Salman Rushdie [or Sir Ahmed Salman Rushdie]
In a Free State (by V.S. Naipaul)
bonuses.leadin_sanitized: This poet's "Christmas in India" is used to exemplify the Eurocentric "representative" stage of writing native literature for an English audience in the post-colonial study "The Empire Writes Back." For 10 points each:
parts_sanitized: Name this British poet whose alignment with colonizers is reflected in the poem "The White Man's Burden."

This author cited the idea of post-colonial authors "writing back" against British colonialism in "The Empire Writes Back with a Vengeance." This author created the fictional Pakistani town of Q in the novel Shame.

As an example of using untranslated words in English literature to "write back," the study examines this short story collection's use of the word "hubshi" to describe a Black maid whom Santosh marries to gain citizenship after he immigrates to Washington, D.C. in the story "One Out of Many."

bonuses.leadin: This character publishes "a very small, brief book of thoughts, Maximes," that all citizens are forced to purchase. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this character who is advised by Raymond and commissions the "State Domain" as president of a country home to characters such as the magician Zabeth. He is known only by a two-word nickname.
Big Man appears in A Bend in the River, a novel by this Trinidadian Nobel laureate who also wrote A House for Mr. Biswas.

A man moves to England to support his brother's engineering studies before learning his brother isn't in school in "Tell Me Who to Kill," a story from this Booker-winning Naipaul book. In its title story, Bobby and Linda drive through the namesake recently independent African country.
answers_sanitized: V.S. Naipaul [or Sir Vidiadhar Surajprasad Naipaul]

bonuses.leadin: A pundit claims this character was born the wrong way and predicts this character will “eat up his mother and father.” For 10 points each:
parts: Name this character who meets his eventual wife Shama while painting signs for the Tulsi family.
This Trinidadian author wrote A House for Mr. Biswas.

After Mr. Biswas steals some of this food item, Jairam forces him to eat the rest until he develops stomach trouble. Miguel Ángel Asturias took almost twenty years to write a trilogy named for this food.
answers_sanitized: V.S. Naipaul [or Sir Vidiadhar Surajprasad Naipaul]

parts_sanitized: Name this character who meets his eventual wife Shama while painting signs for the Tulsi family.
This Trinidadian author wrote A House for Mr. Biswas.

After Mr. Biswas steals some of this food item, Jairam forces him to eat the rest until he develops stomach trouble. Miguel Angel Asturias took almost twenty years to write a trilogy named for this food.

bonuses.leadin: In this novel, a pamphlet claims that “the ancestors shriek” and explains the Liberation Army’s motives for rebelling against a dictator who disbands the Youth Guard. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this novel about Salim, an Indian businessman whose shop is nationalized after a trip to London and whom Ferdinand saves from accusations of selling ivory on the black market.
Salim sees a motto inscribed on a ruined monument near the town’s dock that inverts a quote taken from this ancient Roman poet of The Aeneid.
This Trinidadian author of A Bend in the River also wrote another novel about a man who moves into Hanuman House after marrying Shama, a daughter of the Tulsi family.

This Trinidadian author of A Bend in the River also wrote another novel about a man who moves into Hanuman House after marrying Shama, a daughter of the Tulsi family.

bonuses.leadin: After the title character of this piece asks “what if the baker won’t let me feel the bread,” the narrator responds “are you really going to be the kind of woman the baker won’t let near the bread?” For 10 points each:
parts: Name this prose poem consisting of instructions from a mother to the title character. It begins with the order “Wash the white clothes on Monday and put them in the stone heap.”
“Girl” appears in this author’s collection At the Bottom of the River. This author of Annie John described the title woman’s time as an immigrant au pair in America in the novel Lucy.

This other novelist from the Caribbean wrote about a country ruled by the “Big Man” in A Bend in the River. The title character marries into Shama’s family and tries to obtain financial independence in this Trinidadians’ novel A House for Mr. Biswas.
answers_sanitized: V.S. Naipaul [or Sir Vidiadhar Surajprasad Naipaul]
bonuses.leadin_sanitized: After the title character of this piece asks "what if the baker won't let me feel the bread," the narrator responds "are you really going to be the kind of woman the baker won't let near the bread?" For 10 points each:

This other novelist from the Caribbean wrote about a country ruled by the "Big Man" in A Bend in the River. The title character marries into Shama's family and tries to obtain financial independence in this Trinidadians' novel A House for Mr. Biswas.

bonuses.leadin: This novel’s author claimed that it was inspired by a desire to answer an overheard shout of “What happening there, Bogart?”remembered from his childhood. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this 1959 novel. Residents of its title place include the tailor Bogart, a carpenter working on a “thing without a name” named Popo, an unemployed insane person named Man-Man, and the gambler Hat.
Miguel Street was written by this Indo-Trinidadian author of A House for Mr. Biswas and A Bend in the River.

V.S. Naipaul’s clashes with this Afro-Trinidadian Marxist are described in a biography of the latter by Farrukh Dhondy. This man’s books included the cricket-centered autobiography Beyond a Boundary and a history of the Haitian revolution called The Black Jacobins.
answers_sanitized: C.L.R. James [or Cyril Lionel Robert James]
